definition: Hyperscaler AI capex is funded from operating cash flows (hedge finance in Minsky's taxonomy), structurally different from the debt-funded telecom boom of 2000. Microsoft OCF ~$100B/capex ~$55-65B; Google OCF ~$90-100B/capex ~$50-55B; Amazon OCF ~$85-95B/capex ~$55-65B; Meta OCF ~$65-75B/capex ~$40-45B. All are FCF-positive with combined cash positions ~$300B+.
evidence: Pair_3 debate confirmed both sides agreed on hedge finance classification
open_questions: Does hedge finance status prevent value destruction if ROIC on AI capex stays below WACC?
What triggers voluntary capex rationalization absent the forced liquidation mechanism of debt-funded booms?
Is the Minsky classification adequate or does value-destructive-but-solvent require a new category?
